title: Switching Axis Registry
role: Authoritative vocabulary for the Networking Equipment Selector. The registry enforces MEANING (what can be constrained, how, and which values are legal). It is the SOURCE; the KB schema is a projection of it. Nothing is filterable unless declared here. Each axis declares KIND (ordered | count-at-level | monotonic-capability | discriminating | numeric | boolean) and ROLE (requirement). CONFIG-VARIABLES (config_variables block) are not filter axes: they do not eliminate models, they refine the resolved kitlist (BOM) and are surfaced at the configuration stage. validate-kb.mjs checks model values against this file.
consumers: Web UI builds form controls from this registry (KIND drives the control; ROLE drives WHEN it surfaces).
MCP server (Stage 2) builds tool parameters from this registry.
Reasoning agent (Stage 3) reasons over this registry (via the MCP server).
The KB JSON schema projects typed fields from these axes.
change_policy: Adding a switch model is CHEAP (more data the solver already handles). Adding or changing an AXIS is EXPENSIVE and STRUCTURAL: it touches the UI, the MCP parameters, possibly a new solver condition type. An agent populating models must NEVER add, rename, or re-type an axis on its own — surface it for human ruling. Bump registry_version when the axis set changes.

port_model._comment: v0.4.0 unified port model (encoding B). Replaces the former multigig_*_port_count, access_*_fiber, and uplink_* axes with ONE parametrised axis 'port_count' plus the selector enums below. Port capability is DATA: a model carries model.ports (role=access) and each network module carries ports (role=uplink); a configured variant's port pool = model.ports + the fitted module's ports. Subsumption is the per-port speed SET (a 25G SFP28 port lists [1g,10g,25g]); the solver never infers speed downshift.

port_speed.notes: Ordered low->high. Sub-1G (100M) is intentionally NOT a filter speed (display-only). 'mgig' is no longer a speed: a Multigigabit copper port lists its real speed set, e.g. [1g,2.5g,5g,10g].

config_variables._comment: Dimensions a user PICKS to finalise an orderable kitlist, AFTER the requirement axes have narrowed the model set. They do NOT filter/eliminate models; they resolve which concrete SKUs the BOM shows. Source values live in the KB (license groups). The UI surfaces these at the configuration stage; an MCP server would expose them as kitlist parameters, not filters.

psu_redundancy.notes: Off by default: the kitlist ships a single PSU (configurables.power_supplies.default_primary), and a secondary is added only when a PoE request exceeds what the default primary alone delivers. When ON, the configurator forces a redundant (primary, secondary) pair. Never eliminates a model.

license_term.notes: Subscription term length in years. Available choices vary per license group (some groups carry no term_variable). The pick resolves the concrete subscription SKU (matched on the '-<n>Y' suffix). Never eliminates a model.

axes.name: series
axes.notes: Series is an ordinary axis, not a privileged root. Sub-families are distinct series values. Legal_values will grow as families are added.

axes.name: poe_capable
axes.notes: Discriminating, not monotonic: a non-PoE model is a deliberate (cheaper) choice, so both true and false are meaningful filters. Derivable from poe_type != none; kept explicit for the schema's PoE present-ness conditionals.

axes.name: poe_type
axes.notes: ORDERED level (none<poe<poe+<upoe<upoe+). '>=' means 'this level or better' (a UPOE switch satisfies a poe+ request by downshift); '==' means 'exactly this level' (cost-conscious, don't upsell). level_watts is the per-port standard (the SINGLE SOURCE for the UI/MCP PoE-demand translation: a 'N ports at level L' demand contributes N x level_watts[L] to poe_budget_watts, and max(level) to a poe_type>= constraint). Not a model field.

axes.name: poe_budget_watts
axes.notes: Present only when poe_capable is true. Value = max achievable across the switch's poe_budget_matrix (the PSU-pair delivering a requested wattage is resolved from that matrix at solve time). A UI may gather this as ports x per-level watts instead of a raw total.

axes.name: total_port_count
axes.notes: Physical ACCESS port count. Equals the sum of model.ports[role=access] group counts (validator-checked). Uplinks are NOT included (they come from the fitted module).

axes.name: port_count
axes.notes: PARAMETRISED. A query is {axis:'port_count', where:{role?,medium?,speed}, condition, value}. Resolves against a variant's port pool (model.ports access groups + the fitted module's uplink groups). port_count = number of ports in matching groups whose speeds[] include the requested speed; omitted facets are wildcards. Multiple port_count constraints at different speeds are checked TOGETHER for pool feasibility (shared physical ports), not independently. 'medium:fiber == 0' excludes fiber-access models.

axes.name: uplink_modular
axes.notes: true: uplinks come from a network module (configurables.network_modules group; the fitted member contributes role=uplink ports to a variant). false: fixed uplinks, carried inline in model.ports as role=uplink rows.

axes.name: stacking_capable
axes.notes: Monotonic: presence never hurts. Sensible queries are 'require' (==true) or 'don't care' (omit); '==false' is rarely meaningful and the UI should treat 'not required' as omit, not as ==false.

axes.name: stacking_technology
axes.notes: Required and non-blank on a model when stacking_capable is true. May be left unconstrained in a QUERY.

axes.name: license_regime
axes.notes: Set of regimes a model OFFERS (enum-set). First-class user-selectable input. Couples to switch selection as an ordinary hard constraint (e.g. meraki-subscription -> -M models only). License TERM is a config-variable resolved at the kitlist stage, NOT a filter axis.

axes.name: license_tier
axes.notes: Set of tiers a model OFFERS (enum-set), derived from its license groups: DNA -E -> [essentials], -A -> [advantage], Meraki -M -> [essentials, advantage]. A FILTER on offered-tier capability (parallel to license_regime): choosing 'advantage' drops -E hardware, keeps -A and -M. The essentials-vs-advantage PICK on a model that offers both (Meraki -M) is still a config choice the BOM resolves; this axis only removes models that cannot provide the chosen tier. Stored on the model (mirrors license_regime); validator checks it equals the union of the model's license-group tiers.
